entryPoints.evidence: workflows/notification/ched-pp-workflows.ts:290 — pages.notificationHub.linkAdditionalDetails.click(); page-objects/notification/NotificationHubPage.ts:6-8 — getByText('Additional details', { exact: true }). BOTH RE-VERIFIED EXACT in this review. CAVEAT ADDED IN THIS REVIEW: despite the getter being named linkAdditionalDetails, the locator is getByText, NOT getByRole('link') — contrast the sibling getter linkCommodity at NotificationHubPage.ts:10-12, which DOES use getByRole('link', { name: 'Commodity' }). The hub page itself is out of this page's scope, and no trace of the hub was examined here, so the element type is unconfirmed. The text match, however, is exact and independently corroborates this page's H1. Corroborated by trace 9a918b862ad1b3599fc13659f757c25aaec78ada (the CSV spec), whose gross-weight fill at action 38 has source ched-pp-workflows.ts:292 — i.e. the CSV branch reaches this page via the hub link at :290. entryPoints.description: When an existing CHED-PP is copied to a new draft, the user reaches this page again (via the hub 'Commodity' link, not the linear flow — trace ea99a3b5 action 100). The route itself is CONFIRMED. What is rendered in the gross weight field on this route is value="" — also confirmed. The INTERPRETATION previously attached to that ("copy does not carry the gross weight over") is DOWNGRADED TO inferred IN THIS REVIEW and is CONFOUNDED — see confounds below. Do not treat the non-prefill as an established fact about the copy operation.
entryPoints.confounds: THE NON-PREFILL CLAIM IS NOT SAFE (established in this review by re-deriving the action timeline). Between entering the copied draft and this page rendering, the test MUTATES every commodity line: trace ea99a3b5 action 101 Check on getByRole('checkbox', { name: 'Select all' }), then actions 102-108 overwrite packages ('2'), quantity ('2'), net weight ('10' — up from the original draft's '1') and the dropdowns, then action 109 Click 'Apply' and action 110 Click 'Save and continue', which lands here. So the field's emptiness at action 111 is observed AFTER a commodity mutation, not on a pristine copied draft. Two consequences. (1) The Total table at action 111 reads 'Net weight of the consignment (kg) 20' and 'Number of packages of the consignment 4' — these are the TEST'S OWN re-entered values (10 and 2) applied across two commodity lines by Select all, NOT values carried over by the copy. The previous revision's supporting argument ('copying reproduces the commodity lines — the Total table repopulates — but drops the gross weight') therefore misreads its own evidence: the repopulated numbers are the test's. (The 2x multiple does independently suggest the copied draft arrived with two commodity lines for Select all to act on, which is the one part of that argument that survives.) (2) A competing explanation is at least as plausible and arguably more so: the original draft's gross weight was '12' (action 43) and the mutated net total is 20, so the carried-over gross weight would VIOLATE the GROSS_WEIGHT_GREATER rule (gross must be > net). IPAFFS clearing a now-invalid gross weight on commodity change would produce exactly the observed value="". Distinguishing the two requires a trace that copies a draft and reaches this page WITHOUT touching the commodity lines. No such trace exists in the corpus. This is a question for a human, not something the corpus can settle.
entryPoints.evidence: trace ea99a3b59448073a9924eb56cbc947df8ba082c2 (ched-pp-copy-as-new.spec.ts:15 › CHED-PP copy as new flows › should validate copied CHED-PP after B2C notifier submission and B2B inspection). The gross weight field is filled TWICE in this single trace: action 43 Fill '12' (the original draft) and action 111 Fill '100' (the copied draft), both on internal:role=textbox[name="Total gross weight (kg)"s]. At action 111 the locator resolved to <input value="" ...> — RE-VERIFIED in this review. The empty value attribute is CONFIRMED; the non-prefill INFERENCE drawn from it is not (see confounds). Source of the second fill: ched-pp-workflows.ts:848 via ched-pp-copy-as-new.spec.ts:27. (This trace reports 1 error, but it is at action 155, a Click on 'Record PHSI checks' on a later page — NOT this page. It yields no validation evidence here.) NEW CORROBORATION FROM THE TESTS (this review): the copy route has its OWN config block, separate from DEFAULT_CONFIG — ChedPPCopyAsNewConfig declared at ched-pp-workflows.ts:743 (CORRECTED IN THIS REVIEW: the previous revision cited :745-772; grep puts the interface declaration at :743) and DEFAULT_COPY_CONFIG at :777, whose totalGrossWeightInKgs is '100' (:784, RE-VERIFIED). That is exactly the value seen in the six '100' traces, closing the loop on where that value comes from. The copy workflow's own inline comment at ched-pp-workflows.ts:847 reads `// Commodity Additional Details - gross weight` immediately above the fill at :848 (VERIFIED) — an independent statement by the test authors that this fill lands on THIS page, despite the CommodityExtendedDescriptionPage object being used to drive it. Note also DEFAULT_COPY_CONFIG.netWeightInKgs = '10' (:782) against gross '100', consistent with the gross-greater-than-net rule. Exit on this route differs from the linear one: the copy workflow goes straight from this page's Save and continue to Transport details (:850-852), with no GMS declaration step.

entryPoints.description: Reached from the Review notification page via 'Change commodity details', and NOT directly — the change route lands first on a CHED-PP commodity BULK details page, whose 'Save and continue' then brings the user here. On this route the primary button reads 'Save and review' instead of 'Save and continue' — it returns the user to the review page rather than advancing the linear journey. NOT observed in any trace, and this review established WHY (see below) — it is not a sampling accident.
entryPoints.evidence: workflows/notification/ched-pp-workflows.ts:931 (export async function submitClonedChedPPNotification) — VERIFIED. The three-step approach into this page is :985 pages.reviewNotification.changeCommodityDetails() -> :986 pages.chedppCommodityBulkDetails.updateIntendedUseAndSaveAndContinue() -> :988 pages.commodityAdditionalDetails.enterGrossWeightAndSaveAndReview(fullConfig.grossWeight). CORRECTED IN THIS REVIEW: the previous revision cited :986 and :987 for the first two calls and asserted 'ALL LINE NUMBERS RE-VERIFIED EXACT'; grep puts them at :985 and :986. The :988 citation was correct. The intermediate bulk-details hop at :987 is NEW IN THIS REVIEW — a previous revision described the route as landing here straight from review, which is wrong. page-objects/notification/CommodityAdditionalDetailsPage.ts:22-24 — btnSaveAndReview = getByRole('button', { name: 'Save and review' }); :26-29 — enterGrossWeightAndSaveAndReview, which fills inputTotalGrossWeight (the NON-exact locator at :14-16) then clicks btnSaveAndReview. ALL LINE NUMBERS RE-VERIFIED EXACT in this review. Driven by tests/notification/ched-pp/ched-pp-cloning.spec.ts:10 ('B2C Importer Submits Valid Cloned CHEDPP Notification', tags @release @chedPP @chedPPClone @clone @dynamics) via submitClonedChedPPNotification(pages) at :15 — VERIFIED. WHY IT IS UNTRACED (new, and confirmed on the wire): the corpus DOES contain three traces of this spec — ebd5a5d267ae02ba27210c7c670bbba60a9ff667, 470cd3de7b53a52e16aeb10ef4a69fb1a6a06655 and 7d0bf2f642f2d4eb480f71757b174b90516c49fe — and ALL THREE FAIL EARLY, at the same action: a Click on getByRole('button', { name: 'Clone' }) that times out after 30.0s (ebd5a5d2 action 22; 470cd3de action 24; 7d0bf2f6 action 24 — each opened and re-verified individually in this review). The cloning search never completes, so the run never reaches the Review notification page, let alone this one. The 'Save and review' variant therefore has ZERO rendered evidence anywhere in the corpus for an identifiable reason: the CHED-PP cloning journey is RED in this test run. See openQuestions.

structure.exitRoute: WHERE 'Save and continue' LEADS (new in this review; inferred from the tests, never asserted). On the linear route the next page is CONDITIONAL: ched-pp-workflows.ts:383-386 — `if (gmsDeclaration === yesNo.yes) { await pages.gmsDeclaration.radioConfirmAboveStatement.check(); await pages.gmsDeclaration.btnSaveAndContinue.click(); }` — so a GMS declaration page is interposed when the config opts in; otherwise the journey falls straight through to Transport details at :388-389. DEFAULT_CONFIG.gmsDeclaration = yesNo.no (ched-pp-workflows.ts:159, verified), so the DEFAULT path — and therefore almost every trace — skips GMS and goes to Transport details. On the copy-as-new route there is no GMS branch at all: ched-pp-workflows.ts:849-852 goes from this page's Save and continue directly to Transport details. On the review/cloning route the button is 'Save and review' and returns to the Review notification page (ched-pp-workflows.ts:988-990). All line numbers verified. The exit is therefore three-way and entry-point-dependent — a decision the rebuild must make explicitly.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: IPAFFS-specific class applied alongside govuk-table on the 'Total' table. Suppresses the standard GOV.UK table styling so the table reads as a key/value presentation block rather than a data grid.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Bespoke CSS outside the Design System. It is using a Table to render what is semantically a list of name/value pairs, then restyling it to not look like a table.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Summary list (govuk-summary-list) with no action links — that is the Design System's dedicated component for read-only name/value pairs, and needs no custom CSS.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 41 — <table class="govuk-table presentation-table">; consignmentDetails.html:21. Both verified.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Custom wrapper div around the table, presumably giving it horizontal overflow scrolling on narrow viewports.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Bespoke responsive shim. Unnecessary for a two-column key/value table, and it is not part of govuk-frontend.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Not needed if the table is replaced by a Summary list, which is responsive by default.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 41 — <div class="table-responsive"> wrapping the table; consignmentDetails.html:20. Both verified.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: The 'Back' link has no real destination — href is literally '#', with id=back-link, so navigation is wired up in client-side JS (history.back() or similar).
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Breaks entirely without JavaScript — the link renders but does nothing, which fails GOV.UK's progressive-enhancement baseline. It also gives no meaningful target for 'open in new tab' or for assistive tech announcing the destination. The Design System's Back link guidance is that it should be a real link to the previous page's URL where possible.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Render the Back link with a real server-known href to the previous step, and let JS enhance it if desired. In a linear CHED-PP journey the previous page's URL is always derivable server-side.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 41 — Back. Newly identified in this review; missing from the previous revision, which recorded only backLink: 'Back' with no analysis.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: A non-govuk modifier class on the Back link, positioning it relative to a breadcrumbs region.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Bespoke CSS coupling a Design System component to a layout concern. Note the page renders exactly one breadcrumb-ish container, so the modifier's premise is a layout the new app need not reproduce.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Standard govuk-back-link with no modifier; handle placement with the standard page template's back-link slot.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 41 — class="govuk-back-link back-link--next-to-breadcrumbs"; one element matching [class*=breadcrumb] present on the page. Newly identified in this review.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Renders 'DRAFT.GB.2026.1526011 - CHEDPP' — the draft reference and the CHED type — above the page caption, in a bespoke heading class rather than a Design System component.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Bespoke class named for a heading level ('tertiary') that does not correspond to any govuk-heading-* size, applied to a span rather than a real heading element. Carries load-bearing context (which draft am I editing, what type is it) in markup with no semantic heading role, so it is invisible to heading navigation.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: If this context must persist across the journey, use the caption pattern (govuk-caption-l/xl) already present on the page, or a real heading element at the correct level. The new app should decide deliberately whether a per-page draft-reference banner is needed at all.

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: Error summary title = "Please fix the following errors"
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Default heading text of the IPAFFS validationSummary partial when no headerText is passed.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Deviates from GOV.UK Design System copy, which specifies 'There is a problem' as the Error summary title. Do not carry this wording into the new app.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Error summary with the standard title 'There is a problem'.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/views/partials/common/validationSummary.html:8 — literal 'Please fix the following errors' in the {{else}} branch of {{#if headerText}}; consignmentDetails.html:14 invokes {{> common/validationSummary}} with no headerText argument. Both verified. NOT observed rendered — see openQuestions.

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: Two primary govuk-button submits side by side
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: 'Save and return to hub' and 'Save and continue' are both plain govuk-button with no --secondary modifier, so two equal-weight primary buttons sit next to each other.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Design System guidance is one primary action per page; a secondary action should use govuk-button--secondary or be a link. As rendered there is no visual hierarchy between the two.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-button--secondary on 'Save and return to hub', or a Button group (govuk-button-group) with the secondary action as a link.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 41 — both buttons' full outerHTML re-verified: class="govuk-button govuk-!-margin-right-1 govuk-!-margin-bottom-4" on each, no --secondary modifier on either. Spacing is done with margin utility classes rather than govuk-button-group.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: gross-weight and gross-volume are type=text with maxlength=16, validated server-side as Joi.number().
nonStandardPatterns.concern: No inputmode/numeric affordance; mobile users get an alphabetic keyboard. Length is capped by maxlength rather than by a validation rule, so the cap is silent.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Text input with inputmode="numeric" (Design System guidance for number entry — it deliberately advises against type=number), plus a server-side max-length rule that produces a real error message.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 41 — control inventory confirms both inputs are type=text, maxlength=16, and neither carries an inputmode or pattern attribute; consignmentDetails.html:52,64. Verified.

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: Label text carries the optionality — "Total gross volume (optional)"
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Marks the optional field inline in the label.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: This IS the GOV.UK convention, so it is correct — noted only because the corollary is inconsistent: 'Total gross weight (kg)' carries no '(optional)' suffix yet is ALSO optional in the validator (Joi.number().allow('')). The page therefore implies gross weight is mandatory when it is not.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Either mark gross weight '(optional)' too, or make it genuinely required — the new app should pick one deliberately.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: consignmentDetails.html:49,61; validation/routes/handlers/importer/consignment_details.js:36,121. Rendered labels re-verified in trace e10b7cd5 action 41: exactly 'Total gross weight (kg)' and 'Total gross volume (optional)'. Neither input carries a required attribute (control inventory: required=false on both).

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: One route + template serving four CHED types with mutually exclusive controls
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: The same page renders storage-temperature ('Ambient') and commodity-purpose ('Feedingstuff') radios for CHED-P / CHED-D, but neither for CHED-PP; CHED-PP instead gets the gross volume + unit pair, which the other types do not show. The template branches on isChedpp / isChedp / isNewJourneyChedD throughout, including inside label text.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: The shared page object exposes the union of all four variants' controls, and the tests pick per type. This is heavy runtime branching behind one URL — exactly the architecture the CHED-PP rebuild is meant to avoid. The branching reaches into individual words of copy (see the (kg{{#unless isChedpp}}/units{{/unless}}) label conditionals), which is the most brittle form of it.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: A CHED-PP-only page with only CHED-PP's controls and CHED-PP's literal copy. No fieldConfig/variant gating, no conditionals inside label text.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: page-objects/notification/CommodityAdditionalDetailsPage.ts:6-12 (radioAmbient, radioFeedingstuff) — driven ONLY by workflows/notification/ched-p-workflows.ts:336,717,895 and ched-d-workflows.ts:235-236,622-623; referenced by NO CHED-PP workflow or spec. types/temperature.ts:2 defines ambient='Ambient' as a storage temperature. Template branching verified at consignmentDetails.html:16 ({{#if (and commodityData.totalNetWeight (not (or isNewJourneyChedD isChedp)))}}), :25 and :49 (in-label {{#unless isChedpp}}), :44 ({{#if (and showTotalGrossWeight (not (or isNewJourneyChedD isChedp)))}}). CONFIRMED for CHED-PP: the rendered control inventory at trace e10b7cd5 action 41 contains ZERO radio inputs.

fields.name: gross-weight
fields.label: Total gross weight (kg)
fields.requiredOnSubmitEvidence: legacy — MANDATORY for CHED-PP at the submit/backend layer even though the page form leaves it optional. ipaffs-imports-notification-schema/notification-schema-java/src/main/java/uk/gov/defra/tracesx/notificationschema/representation/Commodities.java:72-80 carries @NotNull(groups={NotificationChedppFieldValidation.class, NotificationSingleCedValidation.class}) on `private BigDecimal totalGrossWeight`, message chedpp.totalgrossweight.not.null='Add the total gross weight' (ValidationMessages.properties:84). This directly ANSWERS openQuestion #2: a CHED-PP is NOT submittable with no total gross weight — the frontend Joi.number().allow('') permits blank on THIS page (confirmed: no `required` attribute in the rendered DOM), but the notification-schema @NotNull blocks submission. Two-layer split (page optional, submit required) — recorded in discrepancies; the rebuild should decide deliberately where to enforce it. validationMessages.evidence: ValidationMessages.properties:90 — `...commodities.gmsdeclarationaccepted.message=Confirm that CDS has asked you to make a GMS application`. validationMessages.trigger: Any validation failure on this page — rendered as the govuk-error-summary__title
validationMessages.evidence: partials/common/validationSummary.html:8 — VERIFIED verbatim as the {{else}} branch of {{#if headerText}}, inside <h2 class="govuk-error-summary__title">. consignmentDetails.html:14 invokes {{> common/validationSummary}} with no headerText argument (verified), so the default fires. Deviates from the Design System's 'There is a problem'. No test asserts this string; the summary did not render in any trace (count 0 at e10b7cd5 action 41).

discrepancies: GROSS WEIGHT MANDATORINESS — page vs submit. The rendered DOM (confirmed) shows gross-weight with NO `required` attribute and the frontend validator allows blank (Joi.number().allow(''), consignment_details.js:36,121), so on THIS page the field is optional. But the notification schema marks it @NotNull for the CHED-PP group (Commodities.java:72-80, message 'Add the total gross weight', ValidationMessages.properties:84), so a CHED-PP cannot be SUBMITTED without it. Not a rendered-vs-legacy contradiction so much as a two-layer split: page-optional, submit-required. The rebuild must decide where the requirement lives. Rendered value kept (required:false at the page level); requiredOnSubmit:true added from legacy.

GROSS-WEIGHT-GREATER-THAN-NET rule — two copy strings for one rule. The frontend page-level message is 'Total gross weight must be greater than the net weight' (validation/messages/en.js:89, GROSS_WEIGHT_GREATER). The backend submit-level message for the same rule is 'Total gross weight is less than the total net weight' (ValidationMessages.properties:83, @MinCommoditiesGrossWeight on the CHED-PP group). Neither was ever observed rendered, so there is no confirmed value to prefer — both are legacy. Recorded so Reconcile can pick ONE canonical wording for the rebuild rather than inheriting the divergence.
openQuestions: No trace in the CHED-PP corpus caught this page in an error state (the error summary is positively confirmed ABSENT from the traced render — .govuk-error-summary count 0), AND no test in the QA automation repo drives it into one — both admissible sources are silent. So EVERY validation message above is `inferred`. Two of the seven are literal strings in the catalogue (GROSS_WEIGHT_GREATER, UNIT_TYPE_REQUIRED) and are high-confidence; the other five are ASSEMBLED at runtime from a template function plus label constants, and while this review verified each template and constant and re-derived each substitution by hand, the exact rendered text should still be confirmed against a real error render before the new app copies it. This page has ZERO negative-path test coverage — worth flagging to the team on its own merits.

Gross weight is NOT required by the validator (Joi.number().allow('')) and the input carries no `required` attribute (confirmed in the rendered DOM), yet the label has no '(optional)' suffix while the sibling gross-volume field does. Every trace fills it, and every test config supplies a value (no test omits it). Is a CHED-PP submittable with no total gross weight, or is it enforced later (e.g. at the review/submit step, or by the backend)? This is a decision the new app must make explicitly.

'Commodity intended for:' — a radio group with inset hint text ('You'll find this on the health or phytosanitary certificate...') is invoked on this template (consignmentDetails.html:83-94) but rendered in NONE of the three traces. THIS REVIEW HARDENS THE ABSENCE CLAIM: the rendered control inventory contains zero radio inputs of any kind, so the radio is confirmed not to render for CHED-PP — it is not merely un-clicked. The internal-market question is answered on an EARLIER page ('About the consignment' — visible in the trace timeline itself at action 29, twelve actions before this page's fill at action 41), which explains why the internalMarket gate is closed here. Remaining question is narrow: is the gate closed for ALL CHED-PP configurations, or only the ones traced? A human should confirm; if confirmed, the field drops from the rebuild's scope.

The gross-weight-decimal validation rule invents a server-side field derived from gross-weight, so its error message ('Gross weight decimal places must be 5 characters or fewer') references a field with no control on the page and no anchor for the error summary link to focus. Likely an accessibility defect in IPAFFS; the new app should express this as a decimal-places rule on the gross weight field itself. Note no test uses a decimal gross weight, so this path is unexercised in automation as well as untraced.

The unit list ('litres', 'metres cubed') is hardcoded in the template, not reference data (verified at consignmentDetails.html:74-76). Note 'metres cubed' is used as both the option value and its display text (verified by enumerating the rendered options), so the stored value is a human-readable string rather than a code (e.g. m3). The new app should decide whether to persist a code instead. The QA repo has no volume-unit vocabulary in types/ (unlike quantity-type.ts / package-type.ts), which is consistent with this being template-hardcoded rather than reference data.

GROSS VOLUME IS COMPLETELY UNEXERCISED BY BOTH SOURCES. It renders for every CHED-PP (traced) but no trace fills it and the QA repo has no locator, no config option and no test for it (zero hits for 'grossvolume' across page-objects/, workflows/, tests/ and types/ — re-verified in this review). So we know the control exists and nothing more: not whether users actually use it, not whether the paired gross-volume/unit required rule works, not what its error states look like. Does CHED-PP genuinely need total gross volume, or is this dead surface inherited from the shared template? A human/BA answer would let the rebuild drop two controls.

The 'Total' table shows net weight and package count computed from the commodity lines, but no gross-weight-derived total. The page's URL carries ?commodityDetailsPage=1 (confirmed on both the GET and the POST — trace e10b7cd5 requests 995 and 1011), implying pagination of commodity details — unclear whether a multi-page commodity set changes what this totals table aggregates. Not exercised: all traces had a single page of commodities, and the tests cap out at three commodities (ched-pp-workflows.ts:347-388), which evidently still fits one page.

DOES COPY AS NEW CARRY THE GROSS WEIGHT OVER? STILL OPEN — AND THE PREVIOUS REVISION'S ANSWER WAS WITHDRAWN IN THIS REVIEW. It asserted as CONFIRMED that 'copy as new does not carry the gross weight over', reasoning that the copied draft's gross weight input is empty while the Total table repopulates. Re-deriving the timeline refutes the reasoning. At trace ea99a3b59448073a9924eb56cbc947df8ba082c2 the test first checks 'Select all' (action 101) and overwrites EVERY commodity line — packages '2', quantity '2', net weight '10' (up from the original draft's '1') — then clicks Apply (109) and Save and continue (110), landing here at 111. So: (i) the Total table's '20' and '4' are the test's own values applied across two lines, NOT copied values — the repopulation argument reads the test's input back as if it were the app's output; (ii) the empty gross weight is observed only AFTER a commodity mutation, never on a pristine copied draft; (iii) the original gross weight ('12', action 43) would be INVALID against the new net total of 20 under the GROSS_WEIGHT_GREATER rule, so 'IPAFFS cleared a now-invalid gross weight' explains the evidence at least as well as 'the copy dropped it'. The corpus cannot separate the two — no trace copies a draft and reaches this page without touching the commodities. The underlying question is real and worth a BA's time (which fields survive a copy, and does changing a commodity silently clear the gross weight?), but it must be asked, not answered from these traces. The one part of the old argument that survives: the 2x multiple implies the copied draft did arrive with two commodity lines for Select all to act on, so commodity lines ARE carried over.

The hidden `etag` field implements optimistic concurrency on the draft. Whether the new app needs equivalent lost-update protection is a design decision worth surfacing, not an IPAFFS implementation detail to discard by default.

The two computed rows have DIFFERENT render gates: the net-weight row sits inside {{#if showTotalGrossWeight}} (consignmentDetails.html:23) while the package-count row does not. So there is a template-reachable state where the Total table shows ONLY a package count and no net weight. Never traced (showTotalGrossWeight was true in all three). Is that state reachable for CHED-PP, and is a lone package-count total meaningful? Newly identified in this review.

The Back link is href="#" and therefore JavaScript-driven (see nonStandardPatterns). No trace clicks it, so where it actually goes — the previous linear step, or the hub when entered from the hub — is unknown. Since this page has THREE entry points (linear, hub link, review/change), 'back' is genuinely ambiguous here and the new app must decide the rule explicitly rather than inheriting history.back(). Newly identified in this review.

CORRECTION TO A PREVIOUS FINDING (was: 'the page object's Save and review button belongs to the CHED-D/CHED-P variants'). That is WRONG for the button. A CHED-PP workflow DOES use it: submitClonedChedPPNotification calls commodityAdditionalDetails.enterGrossWeightAndSaveAndReview (ched-pp-workflows.ts:988 — verified), i.e. when this page is entered from the Review notification page via 'Change commodity details', the primary button reads 'Save and review' and returns to review instead of advancing. No trace in our corpus captured this variant. The rest of that finding STANDS and is now positively corroborated: radioAmbient/radioFeedingstuff (CommodityAdditionalDetailsPage.ts:6-12) are driven ONLY by ched-p/ched-d workflows and never by CHED-PP, and zero radios render for CHED-PP. So: the button-label variance is a REAL CHED-PP requirement the rebuild must decide on (does a change-from-review flow return to review?); the radios are genuinely out of scope.

THE CHED-PP CLONING JOURNEY IS RED IN THIS TEST RUN, AND THAT IS WHY THE 'Save and review' VARIANT OF THIS PAGE HAS NO RENDERED EVIDENCE. Newly established in this review. The corpus contains three traces of ched-pp-cloning.spec.ts:10 (ebd5a5d267ae02ba27210c7c670bbba60a9ff667, 470cd3de7b53a52e16aeb10ef4a69fb1a6a06655, 7d0bf2f642f2d4eb480f71757b174b90516c49fe — the latter two are retries). All three fail identically: a Click on getByRole('button', { name: 'Clone' }) times out after 30.0s, so the run dies on the cloning summary page and never reaches Review notification, never reaches this page. Two consequences worth separating. (1) FOR THIS SPEC: the review/change entry route, the 'Save and review' button label, and the 1001 gross-weight value are all `inferred` for a KNOWN reason rather than an unknown one — no amount of re-reading the corpus will confirm them, and a human must either fix the cloning test or answer the question directly. (2) FOR THE TEAM, independently of the rebuild: three consecutive failures on a @release-tagged spec is a broken journey or a broken test, and it means the whole 'change something from the review page' pattern — not just on this page — is unverified in this run. Worth raising on its own merits.

This page's exit is THREE-WAY and depends on the entry route: linear -> GMS declaration if gmsDeclaration is Yes (ched-pp-workflows.ts:383-386) else Transport details (:388); copy-as-new -> always Transport details, no GMS branch (:849-852); review/change -> back to Review notification via 'Save and review' (:988-990). Newly identified in this review. DEFAULT_CONFIG.gmsDeclaration is 'No' (:159), so the GMS interstitial is skipped in nearly every trace and we have almost no evidence about it from this page's vantage point. Combined with the Back link being href="#" (see nonStandardPatterns), BOTH directions of navigation from this page are route-dependent and neither is expressed in the markup — the new app must make both explicit rather than inheriting them.

The CHED-PP tests drive THIS page's gross weight field through the CommodityExtendedDescriptionPage object (inputTotalGrossWeightinKgs — CommodityExtendedDescriptionPage.ts:55-57), not through CommodityAdditionalDetailsPage, except on the cloning route. The accessibility suite likewise labels this page 'Commodity Extended Description Page (gross weight)'. The QA repo has therefore blurred two adjacent pages into one page object. This is a note for whoever reads the test suite as documentation — it is a mislabelling in the tests, NOT evidence that the two pages are one. The traces are unambiguous that 'Additional details' is its own page with its own URL (.../consignment/details?commodityDetailsPage=1, verified on the wire) and its own H1 ('Additional details', verified in the DOM). Consequence worth noting: CommodityAdditionalDetailsPage.inputTotalGrossWeight uses a NON-exact locator (name: 'Total gross weight') while CommodityExtendedDescriptionPage.inputTotalGrossWeightinKgs uses exact: true with '(kg)' — two locators for one field, only one of which pins the label. SHARPENED IN THIS REVIEW: the blurring is visible inside a single seven-line block — ched-pp-workflows.ts:289 comments `// Additional Details Page`, then :291 comments `// Commodity Extended Description Page` immediately above the gross-weight fill at :292. The test authors contradict themselves about which page they are on. Countervailing evidence that they DO know: the copy-as-new workflow's comment at :847 reads `// Commodity Additional Details - gross weight` above the same fill — correctly attributing it to this page while still using the wrong page object. So the page object choice is a known shortcut, not a misunderstanding.

COPY-AS-NEW HAS ITS OWN CONFIG BLOCK WITH DIFFERENT DEFAULTS, AND NOBODY HAS SAID WHY. Newly identified in this review. ChedPPCopyAsNewConfig / DEFAULT_COPY_CONFIG (ched-pp-workflows.ts:745-792) sets totalGrossWeightInKgs '100' against netWeightInKgs '10', where the linear DEFAULT_CONFIG sets '12' against '1'. Both satisfy gross>net, so neither is testing a boundary. The divergence looks incidental rather than meaningful, but if it IS meaningful — e.g. a copied consignment is expected to be re-weighed at a realistic scale — that is a requirement nobody has written down. Cheap question for the QA author; the answer decides whether the rebuild's copy flow needs distinct test data at all.
